data.title: Pennpetro delays 2024/25 results to clean up legacy audit issues
data.description:
Claim 70% Off TipRanks Premium

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Stay ahead of the market with the latest news and analysis and maximize your portfolio's potential

Pennpetro Energy Plc ( (GB:PPP) ) has shared an announcement.

Pennpetro Energy has opted to delay publication of its audited 2024/25 full-year accounts by one to two weeks to incorporate recently recovered historic accounting documents. The company says the additional time will allow auditors to reduce the extent of disclaimers of opinion in the accounts, addressing legacy gaps that had previously undermined audit quality and market confidence.

Management chose not to meet an earlier self-imposed deadline that would have resulted in accounts carrying heavier qualifications and unresolved issues. Executive chairman Richard Spinks framed the short delay as essential to improving audit clarity, supporting the company’s going-concern assessment and facilitating its efforts to lift the current trading suspension and progress strategic development plans.

More about Pennpetro Energy Plc

Pennpetro Energy Plc is an energy company focused on developing strategic traditional and transition energy projects. The group is working to restore compliance and strengthen governance following historic accounting and documentation issues that pre-date the current leadership.

Average Trading Volume: 512,108

Technical Sentiment Signal: Sell

Current Market Cap: £9.57M

For a thorough assessment of PPP stock, go to TipRanks’ Stock Analysis page.

data.title: Sturm Ruger Outlines 2026 Strategy After Tough 2025
data.description:
Claim 70% Off TipRanks Premium

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Stay ahead of the market with the latest news and analysis and maximize your portfolio's potential

Sturm Ruger & Company ( (RGR) ) has provided an update.

Sturm, Ruger & Company reported that 2025 was a difficult year for the firearms and broader consumer durables industries, but the company managed modest top-line growth and market share gains, with estimated sell-through up 4.5% despite a 4.1% decline in adjusted NICS background checks. On March 2, 2026, executives detailed that full-year net sales rose 1.9% to $546 million while the company swung to a net loss of $0.27 per share due to nonrecurring costs tied to leadership transition, product rationalization and organizational realignment, though it remained debt-free, generated $54 million in operating cash flow, invested heavily in capacity and innovation, and returned $36 million to shareholders via dividends and buybacks.

Management highlighted that adjusted 2025 earnings were $0.84 per share, supported by strong demand for new launches, including 65 models and three new platforms such as the Glenfield by Ruger rifle and Red Label III shotgun. Looking ahead, the company outlined a 2026 plan centered on sustaining revenue in a flat-to-down market, expanding operating margins through structural efficiencies, accelerating innovation in both firearms and accessories, and increasing production capacity under its Ruger 2030 strategy, moves that signal an emphasis on long-term profitable growth and competitive positioning despite industry headwinds.

The most recent analyst rating on (RGR) stock is a Hold with a $40.00 price target. To see the full list of analyst forecasts on Sturm Ruger & Company stock, see the RGR Stock Forecast page.

Spark’s Take on RGR Stock

According to Spark, TipRanks’ AI Analyst, RGR is a Neutral.

The score is primarily supported by a conservative, low-risk balance sheet and solid ongoing cash generation, plus a constructive (but not strongly bullish) technical setup. These positives are tempered by materially weaker profitability highlighted in both the financial statements and earnings call, and a very stretched valuation (P/E ~289) that raises downside risk if earnings do not recover.

To see Spark’s full report on RGR stock, click here.

More about Sturm Ruger & Company

Sturm, Ruger & Company, Inc. is a U.S. firearms manufacturer specializing in rifles, shotguns and handguns for the civilian and sporting markets. The company focuses on product innovation, category leadership and an expanding ecosystem of higher-margin accessories aimed at both core and emerging firearm consumers.

Average Trading Volume: 275,256

Technical Sentiment Signal: Buy

Current Market Cap: $609.2M

The latest update is out from Character ( (GB:CCT) ).

The Character Group plc has repurchased 8,500 of its ordinary shares at 244 pence each under its existing share buyback programme, with the shares subsequently cancelled. Following this transaction, the company now holds 1,983,059 shares in treasury and has 17,551,149 shares in issue, a figure that sets the new baseline for shareholder voting rights and regulatory disclosure thresholds.

The buyback, executed via Panmure Liberum on AIM, modestly reduces the company’s free float and can be seen as a capital management move that may enhance earnings per share and signal management confidence in the business. The updated total voting rights will guide investors in assessing their reportable holdings under the FCA’s Disclosure Guidance and Transparency Rules.

Georgia Capital PLC ( (GB:CGEO) ) has issued an announcement.

Georgia Capital PLC has continued its ongoing share buyback and cancellation programme, with Deutsche Bank’s London branch purchasing 10,000 ordinary shares on its behalf on 4 March 2026 at a volume-weighted average price of 3,706.1849 pence. The trades, executed on the London Stock Exchange, ranged between 3,620 and 3,732.5 pence per share and are intended to reduce the company’s share capital over time.

Following this latest transaction, Georgia Capital now holds 40,000 ordinary shares in treasury, with 34,664,357 voting rights remaining in issue out of a total 34,704,357 ordinary shares. The latest update is out from Funding Circle Holdings ( (GB:FCH) ).

Funding Circle Holdings has continued its ongoing share buy-back programme, purchasing 63,008 ordinary shares on the London Stock Exchange on 4 March 2026 at prices between 140.00p and 146.00p, with a volume-weighted average price of 143.9863p. The company intends to hold these shares in treasury, bringing its treasury holding to 4,473,277 shares and leaving 300,268,299 shares in issue, which now represents the total voting rights used for regulatory disclosure thresholds.

data.title: AmBase Secures New Litigation Funding Amid Liquidity Strain
data.description:
Claim 70% Off TipRanks Premium

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Stay ahead of the market with the latest news and analysis and maximize your portfolio's potential

An announcement from AmBase ( (ABCP) ) is now available.

On March 2, 2026, AmBase entered into a new litigation funding agreement with Chairman and CEO Richard A. Bianco, under which he will provide up to $6 million, including converting $4 million of existing promissory notes, to finance the company’s operations and ongoing litigation over the 111 West 57th property, with detailed tiered participation for Bianco in any future litigation recoveries. Separately, BARC Investments exercised its option to convert a $2 million note issued in August 2024 into a pari passu litigation funding arrangement, further reducing AmBase’s share of any eventual litigation proceeds as the company continues to face going-concern uncertainties and explores additional financing and strategic alternatives to meet its capital needs.

The Bianco agreement immediately injects $1 million for litigation expenses and another $1 million for working capital and related costs while extending the maturity of accrued interest, thereby providing short-term liquidity but at the cost of granting Bianco a significant priority claim on future recoveries. The BARC conversion similarly preserves cash by deferring cash repayment in favor of sharing in potential litigation proceeds on the same terms as Bianco’s funding, underscoring that AmBase’s ability to continue operating and realize value from its 111 West 57th investment now depends heavily on securing further capital and achieving successful outcomes in its complex, long-running disputes.

The most recent analyst rating on (ABCP) stock is a Sell with a $0.20 price target. To see the full list of analyst forecasts on AmBase stock, see the ABCP Stock Forecast page.

Spark’s Take on ABCP Stock

According to Spark, TipRanks’ AI Analyst, ABCP is a Neutral.

The score is driven primarily by very weak financial performance (persistent losses, negative equity, and cash burn). Technicals are neutral-to-weak with price below longer-term averages and negative MACD. Valuation offers limited support due to losses (negative P/E) and no dividend, while recent financing-related corporate events add further downside risk via going-concern and funding uncertainty.

To see Spark’s full report on ABCP stock, click here.

More about AmBase

AmBase Corporation is a holding company whose primary asset is a disputed equity investment in the 111 West 57th Street real estate project in New York, and its current operations are largely focused on pursuing related litigation. The company has repeatedly warned of substantial doubt about its ability to continue as a going concern and is actively seeking capital through litigation funding, debt and equity financing to sustain operations and protect the value of its investment.

Average Trading Volume: 20,093

Technical Sentiment Signal: Sell

Current Market Cap: $14.55M

data.description:
Claim 50% Off TipRanks Premium

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions

The latest announcement is out from Residential Secure Income ( (GB:RESI) ).

Residential Secure Income plc reported that all resolutions at its Annual General Meeting on 4 March 2026 were approved by shareholders via poll. The outcome confirms strong investor support for the company’s governance, financial reporting and ongoing strategy.

Shareholders backed the receipt of the 2025 Annual Report and Accounts, the directors’ remuneration policy and report, and the re-election of all named directors. They also renewed authorities for share issuance, pre-emption right disapplications, share buybacks and shorter notice for general meetings, providing the board with flexibility over capital management and corporate actions.

The meeting reappointed BDO LLP as auditor and authorised the board to set its fees, underscoring continuity in financial oversight. Just under half of the issued share capital, excluding treasury shares, was voted, indicating a solid level of engagement from the investor base despite some minority opposition on certain resolutions such as director re-election and additional pre-emption disapplications.

data.title: GE Healthcare Advances Sonazoid Trial in Paediatric Liver Imaging: What Investors Should Watch
data.description:
Ge Healthcare Technologies Inc. (GEHC) announced an update on their ongoing clinical study.

Claim 70% Off TipRanks Premium

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Stay ahead of the market with the latest news and analysis and maximize your portfolio's potential

GE Healthcare is running a Phase 4 study called “A Phase 4, Open-Label, Non-randomised, Multicentre Study to Evaluate Safety and Efficacy of Intravenous Administration of Sonazoid™ for Contrast-Enhanced Ultrasound Liver Imaging in Paediatric Patients.” The goal is to see how well Sonazoid works and how safe it is for spotting and characterising liver tumours in children, an area with clear unmet need.

The study tests Sonazoid, a contrast agent made of tiny perfluorobutane microbubbles used during ultrasound scans. Its purpose is to sharpen liver images so doctors can better tell if a child’s liver mass is benign or malignant, while avoiding more invasive scans and reducing exposure to radiation or complex imaging workflows.

This is an interventional Phase 4 trial with a single treatment group, where all enrolled children receive Sonazoid-enhanced ultrasound. The study is open label with no randomisation or masking, and the main purpose is diagnostic, meaning the focus is on image quality, tumour characterisation, and safety rather than treatment outcomes.

The trial is currently recruiting, reflecting active patient enrolment and site engagement. The study was first submitted on 1 October 2024 and the latest update on 3 March 2026 signals recent protocol confirmation or operational changes, though primary and final completion dates have not yet been posted for investors to track readout timing.

For GE Healthcare, positive data could support broader paediatric use of Sonazoid and help drive adoption of contrast-enhanced ultrasound systems, adding a differentiated imaging tool to its portfolio. This may lift investor sentiment around GEHC’s imaging franchise, while also pressuring rivals in paediatric liver imaging that rely more on CT or MRI contrast agents.

If safety and performance are favourable, hospitals could accelerate shifts toward ultrasound-based liver imaging in children, which may deepen recurring revenue from contrast sales and ultrasound platforms for GE Healthcare. Near term, the impact on GEHC’s share price may be modest, but steady progress in niche, high-value indications can strengthen the long-term growth story in diagnostic imaging and support valuation multiples.

The Sonazoid paediatric liver imaging study remains active and recently updated, with further operational and scientific details available on the ClinicalTrials portal.

To learn more about GEHC’s potential, visit the Ge Healthcare Technologies Inc. drug pipeline page.
